claim: The Currier A/B split reflects a genuine linguistic difference between two distinct languages or dialects.
evidence_for: Currier A has higher hapax ratio compared to Currier B
Word-final glyph distribution in Currier A is more consistent with Latin word endings
evidence_against: Other sections do not show similar patterns
test: Kullback-Leibler divergence between word-final glyph distributions of Currier A and B

tests_run.test: kullback-leibler divergence between word-final glyph distributions of currier a and b
tests_run.details: Language A: entropy=3.8321, 3513 unique/11022 total words. Language B: entropy=3.8611, 5089 unique/23766 total words. Entropy difference: 0.0290. Vocabulary density difference: 0.1046.
